title: hermannia engleri
intro: Hermannia engleri is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the red-berried wild rose or wild hibiscus, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hermannia engleri include its ability to treat fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-constipation remedy. Hermannia engleri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
